slice: Duskmourn cluster — 6 trivia-passed + 3 wave-5 vision-only cards. Caller's prompt listed Fear-of cycle, Valgavoth character, Duskmourn set hub, and several 1:1 candidates. Caller misstated Fear-of count as 2; both Edgelords independently corrected it to 5 (DSK-9, 55, 56, 134, 135).

agreed_move.shape: n/a — both Edgelords proposed, but on disjoint slices of the cluster

divergences.opus_value: DSK-9, DSK-55, DSK-56, DSK-134, DSK-135 (Fear-of cycle, 5 cards)
divergences.sonnet_value: DSK-189, DSK-190 (Manifest Dread + Moldering Gym/Weight Room, 2 cards)
divergences.judge_note: Disjoint. Zero card overlap. Each Edgelord picked a different load-bearing move within the same cluster. This is not contradiction; it is two non-colliding selections from the candidate pool the caller's prompt offered.

divergences.opus_value: stack (node-proposal anchoring 5 cards via characters: [fear-of-cycle] pointer)
divergences.sonnet_value: mirror (1:1 bidirectional edge written as two MD bullets, one in each card)
divergences.judge_note: Different shapes by necessity — Opus's 5-card cycle cannot be a 1:1 mirror without writing 10 bullets at an absent center (Opus correctly identifies this in his description); Sonnet's 2-card pair is exactly the 1:1 base case. The shape divergence reflects the cardinality of the underlying canonical fact each Edgelord chose to model, not a methodological disagreement.

divergences.opus_value: Transformed (Mr. Nodeley took the reins; created cards/_characters/fear-of-cycle.md)
divergences.sonnet_value: Did not transform (held Edgelord posture; flagged the Fear-of cycle in other_candidates_considered as 'strongest Nodeley candidate in this batch, recommend a dedicated Mr. Nodeley pass')
divergences.judge_note: Both agents identified the Fear-of cycle as Nodeley-territory. Opus chose to act on it in-pass; Sonnet chose to flag it for a future pass and prosecute the cleaner 1:1 instead. Both choices are defensible under the Edgelord spec. The dual-pipeline pattern is functioning exactly as intended: two reasoners surface two different valid moves; the parent picks one or both.
Cosmetic. Each mood matches its respective shape — Nodeley pride for the Nodeley transformation, professional sigh for the routine 1:1 mirror. No semantic conflict.

recommended_implementation: Commit BOTH proposals. They are non-colliding, both rigorous, both designer-confirmed-cycle, both already written to disk on disjoint files (Opus: fear-of-cycle.md + 5 Fear-of card frontmatter pointers; Sonnet: 189 + 190 MD bullets). The 'pick stronger' enum value is the closest spec-fit, but the substantive recommendation is 'pick BOTH because the divergence is geographic (different cards) not philosophical (same cards, different read).' If the parent must pick one to commit and one to roll back, the Fear-of cycle node (Opus) is the higher-leverage move because it (a) creates a load-bearing graph anchor for 3 currently-stub cards that have no other attachment, (b) gives the bundler a pre-built cohesion stack for a future 'Fears of Duskmourn' Discrete Lair, and (c) follows the established Endriders collective-entity precedent. Sonnet's 1:1 mirror is also correct but is a leaf-level edge with no downstream graph multiplier — easier to re-propose in a future pass if rolled back now. So: keep both if the parent has appetite; if rolling back one, roll back Sonnet's 1:1 (lower regret).
judge_notes: Both Edgelords read the same cluster brief, both caught the caller's miscount of the Fear-of cycle (5, not 2), both identified the Manifest-Dread ↔ Moldering-Gym oracle-text dependency, both identified the Fear-of cycle as a 5-card Nodeley-grade structural fact. They diverged on which move to prosecute first — Opus took the cycle node, Sonnet took the 1:1 mirror — and each explicitly acknowledged the other's chosen move in their alternatives_considered list (Sonnet recommending the Fear-of node as 'strongest Nodeley candidate in this batch'; Opus flagging Manifest-Dread ↔ Moldering-Gym as 'mechanically clean and designer-confirmed' but preferring a future keyword-cycle node over the 1:1). The divergence is structural-low / evidentiary-high: the agents agree on every underlying fact and on the category, they only disagree on the shape and scope of the move they personally chose to write. This is the cleanest possible flavor of divergence — two valid moves on different slices of the same cluster, neither contradicting the other, both fully-cited, both already committed to disk on non-overlapping files. The parent should keep both. If escalation to Alex is desired the framing is 'we got two different correct answers; both shipped; do you want either of them rolled back' — and the answer almost certainly is no.
